This isolates \( [[a]]x^{3} \) but neither divides by \( [[a]]\) nor takes the cube root.

stem: Let \( f(x) = [[a]]x + ([[b]]) \). If \( f^{-1}(x) = mx + k \) for constants \( m \) and \( k \), what is \( m + k \)?
solution: Swap \( x \) and \( y \) in \( y = [[a]]x + ([[b]]) \) and solve for \( y \): \( x = [[a]]y + ([[b]]) \Rightarrow y = \dfrac{1}{[[a]]}x - \dfrac{[[b]]}{[[a]]} \). So \( m = \dfrac{1}{[[a]]} \) and \( k = -\dfrac{[[b]]}{[[a]]} \), giving \( m + k = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports only the slope \( m \) of the inverse, forgetting to add the intercept \( k \).
This reports only the intercept \( k \), forgetting to add the slope \( m \).
This confuses the inverse's coefficients with the original function's coefficients \( a \) and \( b \) themselves.
This flips the sign of the intercept term \( k \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: Set \( \dfrac{[[a]]x + ([[b]])}{[[c]]x + ([[d]])} = [[y0]] \), cross-multiply to get \( [[a]]x + ([[b]]) = [[y0]]\big([[c]]x + ([[d]])\big) \), and collect the \( x \) terms on one side to solve: \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This just restates the given output value \( [[y0]] \) instead of solving for \( x \).
This negates the correct value (a sign slip when isolating \( x \)).
This cross-multiplies but uses the wrong side's coefficients when isolating \( x \).
This swaps which coefficients multiply \( y_0 \) versus which stand alone, a common bookkeeping slip.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

solution: Set \( [[a]]\sqrt{x-([[h]])} + ([[k]]) = [[y0]] \), isolate the radical as \( \sqrt{x-([[h]])} = \dfrac{[[y0]] - ([[k]])}{[[a]]} \), then square both sides: \( x = [[h]] + \left(\dfrac{[[y0]]-([[k]])}{[[a]]}\right)^{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This isolates the radical correctly but forgets to square it before adding to \( [[h]] \).
This subtracts the squared term instead of adding it.
This squares the isolated radical correctly but forgets to add \( [[h]] \).
This reports the domain's left endpoint \( [[h]] \) rather than solving for the actual input.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: Since \( x \ge [[h]] \) is the restricted (invertible) branch, take the \( + \) square root when solving \( (x-([[h]]))^{2} = [[y0]] - ([[k]]) \): \( x = [[h]] + \sqrt{[[y0]] - ([[k]])} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This takes the \( - \) square root, which belongs to the excluded branch \( x < [[h]] \).
This forgets to take the square root at all, using the difference directly.
This finds the offset from \( [[h]] \) but forgets to add \( [[h]] \) itself back.
This squares the difference instead of taking its square root.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: Set \( [[a]]^{x} + ([[b]]) = [[y0]] \), isolate the exponential as \( [[a]]^{x} = [[y0]] - ([[b]]) \), then take \( \log_{[[a]]} \) of both sides: \( x = \log_{[[a]]}\!\big([[y0]] - ([[b]])\big) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to take the logarithm, leaving the isolated power itself.
This forgets to subtract \( [[b]] \) before taking the logarithm.
This divides by \( [[a]] \) instead of taking a logarithm base \( [[a]] \).
This negates the correct exponent (a sign slip).
This is off by one from the correct value.
